output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's What to Do (and Fast)
output.meta_description: Landlord withholding your security deposit? Know your tenant rights, state deadlines, and how a demand letter gets your money back in 60 seconds.
content_outline: Introduction: My Landlord Won't Give Me My Deposit Back—Now What?
Can a Landlord Keep Your Security Deposit? Understanding the Law
What Can a Landlord Deduct from Your Security Deposit vs. Normal Wear and Tear
How Long Does a Landlord Have to Return a Deposit? State-by-State Deadlines
What to Do When Your Landlord Is Not Returning Your Deposit: A 4-Step Action Plan
Send a Security Deposit Dispute Letter That Gets Results
Conclusion: Reclaim Your Deposit in 60 Seconds—No Sign-Up Required
key_points: Most U.S. states require landlords to return security deposits within 14–60 days and provide an itemized list of any deductions.
Landlords cannot deduct for normal wear and tear; only damage beyond ordinary use or unpaid rent qualifies.
A formal security deposit demand letter is often the fastest, most effective escalation step before filing a small claims dispute.
Deposits-Back.com generates a legally formatted demand letter in 60 seconds for a flat $19.99 with no subscription or sign-up.
output.cta: Don't let your landlord keep what's yours. Generate your security deposit demand letter in 60 seconds for just $19.99 at Deposits-Back.com—no sign-up, no subscription required.
output.target_audience: Frustrated U.S. renters whose landlord is withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit and who need a fast, affordable way to demand their money back.
